title: Newar Forrth

'''Newar Forrth''', or '''New'arforrth''' in his [[Twi'leki/Legends|native tongue]], was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] serving the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the latter decades of the [[Republic Dark Age]]. The leader of the [[Nercathi clan]], Forrth was a [[Jedi Lord]] on [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]], ruling his constituents in return for protecting the planet from the [[New Sith]].

==Biography==
===Jedi Lord===
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] of the [[Nercathi clan]], Newar Forrth was born on [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Republic Dark Age|Dark Age]], a time of [[New Sith Wars|war]] and suffering caused by the actions of the [[New Sith|Sith Empire]]. [[Jedi training/Legends|Trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], Forrth became a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and later the [[Jedi Lord|Lord]] of his homeworld in exchange for protecting the Ryloth citizens from the Sith. Fashioning two [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] for his personal use, Forrth was a master of the two-bladed [[Jar'Kai/Legends|Jar'Kai]] style of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]].<ref name="DFS4" />

In [[1010 BBY]], the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council's]] failed protégé [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Skere Kaan]] broke away from the Order and created the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] to destroy the Order and the Republic. Upon hearing [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple's]] [[Battlemaster/Legends|battlemaster]] [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Lord Hoth]] had rallied the banners of the [[Jedi Grand Council]], Lord Forrth mustered those Jedi and commoners loyal to him and joined Hoth's [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]]. Fighting for a decade against Kaan's Brotherhood, Forrth was charged with leading the [[Third Legion of Light]] on such campaigns as the triumphant [[Battle of Malrev IV|battle]] at [[Malrev IV]].<ref name="DFS4" />

In the last year of the fighting, Lord Hoth led Forrth and the other great lords on a [[Ruusan campaign|final campaign]] on the fields of [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]. Fighting alongside Hoth, Lord Forrth was struck down during the long period of fighting, perishing on the muddy world with so many of his brethren.<ref name="DFS4" />

===Legacy===
Following Lord Forrth's death, the battle of Ruusan was eventually won and the Sith forces vanquished a heavy cost. Following the Republic and the Jedi Order's enactment of the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformations]], Jedi Knight [[Johun Othone]] was able to convince the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] to grant him the funds necessary to construct the massive [[Valley of the Jedi]] as a memorial on Ruusan. Having recovered Forrth's body, the Jedi [[Jedi funeral/Legends|cremated]] the Twi'lek's remains and sent them to Ryloth, while three statues were erected in the Valley in memory of the fallen Lord. On Ryloth, members of his clan used the ashes of Forrth's corporeal form to fashion three crystal statues of their own.<ref name="DFS4" />

Over a millennium after his death, aspiring young Twi'lek Force-sensitive [[Boc Aseca]] found the crystal statues of Forrth on Ryloth. Using his [[Psychometry/Legends|psychometric]] abilities, Aseca gleaned memories of the battles at Ruusan from the statues, thus fueling his descent to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Years later, Aseca traveled to Ruusan and the Valley of the Jedi where he met [[Kyle Katarn]] in battle beneath the ancient statues of Forrth. After a fierce [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] Aseca was cut down in the shadow of the great Twi'lek Jedi.<ref name="DFS4" />

==Behind the scenes==
The inspiration for Forrth's remains being shaped into three idols is from ''[[Idol Intentions]]'' in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 12|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 12]].<ref name="tfn">{{JCFCite|url=posts/20023080|board=Literature|thread=Kyle Katarn's Tale (Dark Forces Articles by Abel & Jason)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2005-06-01|time=1:26 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801144548/https://web.archive.org/screenshot/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/kyle-katarns-tale-dark-forces-articles-by-abel-jason.19114424/page-13}}</ref>
